sensitivity: 0.79 overall (standalone); 22.4% improvement in aided vs. unaided reading on instance level
auc: 8.7% increase in overall AUC from control to study arm (p<0.001)
intended_use: DTX Studio Clinic is a computer assisted detection (CADe) device that analyses intraoral radiographs to identify and localize dental findings, which include caries, calculus, periapical radiolucency,
intended_use_full: DTX Studio Clinic is a computer assisted detection (CADe) device that analyses intraoral radiographs to identify and localize dental findings, which include caries, calculus, periapical radiolucency, root canal filling deficiency, discrepancy at margin of an existing restoration and bone loss. The DTX Studio Clinic CADe functionality is indicated for use by dentists for the concurrent review of bitewing and periapical radiographs of permanent teeth in patients 15 years of age or older.
target_condition: dental findings including caries, calculus, periapical radiolucency, root canal filling deficiency, discrepancy at margin of existing restoration, and bone loss
ground_truth: Ground truth defined by 4 dental practitioners with at least 20 years of experience reading dental x-rays, using 3 out of 4 consensus; for standalone testing: 10 dental practitioners followed by expert review
other_metrics: Standalone precision: 0.45 (95% CI [0.40, 0.50]); Standalone sensitivity per finding type: caries 0.70, periapical radiolucency 0.68, root canal filling deficiency 0.95, discrepancy at restoration margin 0.82, bone loss 0.78, calculus 0.80; Mean Dice score range: 53.5% to 71.9%; Mean IoU range: 44.8% to 58.6%; AUC improvements by finding type: root canal filling deficiency +13.5%, periapical radiolucency +10.2%, discrepancy at margin +10.1%, calculus +7.2%, caries +6.1%, bone loss +5.6%; Instance-level sensitivity improvements: caries +19.6%, bone loss +23.5%, calculus +18.1%, discrepancy at margin +28.5%, periapical radiolucency +20.6%, root canal filling deficiency +27.4%; Specificity decrease (aided vs. unaided): 8.7% overall (95% CI [6.6, 10.7])
